message:
Add crew rename, pristine commands and status integration

- gt crew rename: Rename crew workspace (kills session, renames dir)
- gt crew pristine: Sync crew workspaces with git pull + bd sync
- gt status: Show crew workers per rig with 👤 indicator

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"gt-9a2.8","title":"CloudRunOutpost: Basic implementation","description":"## Overview

Basic CloudRunOutpost implementation. Persistent connections and cost tracking are separate tasks.

## Implementation

```go
type CloudRunOutpost struct {
    name        string
    project     string
    region      string
    service     string
    maxWorkers  int
    client      *WorkClient
    workers     map[string]*CloudRunWorker
    mu          sync.RWMutex
}

func NewCloudRunOutpost(cfg OutpostConfig) (*CloudRunOutpost, error) {
    serviceURL := fmt.Sprintf(
        "https://%s-%s.a.run.app",
        cfg.Service, cfg.Region,
    )
    return &CloudRunOutpost{
        name:       cfg.Name,
        project:    cfg.Project,
        region:     cfg.Region,
        service:    cfg.Service,
        maxWorkers: cfg.MaxWorkers,
        client:     NewWorkClient(serviceURL),
        workers:    make(map[string]*CloudRunWorker),
    }, nil
}
```

## Spawn

```go
func (o *CloudRunOutpost) Spawn(issue string, cfg WorkerConfig) (Worker, error) {
    req := WorkRequest{
        IssueID: issue,
        Rig:     RigConfig{URL: cfg.RigURL, Branch: cfg.GitBranch},
        Beads:   BeadsConfig{URL: cfg.BeadsURL, Branch: "beads-sync"},
        Branch:  "polecat/" + issue,
    }
    
    events, err := o.client.DispatchWork(context.Background(), req)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    worker := &CloudRunWorker{
        id:      uuid.New().String(),
        outpost: o.name,
        issue:   issue,
        events:  events,
        status:  WorkerStatusWorking,
    }
    
    o.mu.Lock()
    o.workers[worker.id] = worker
    o.mu.Unlock()
    
    go worker.monitor()
    return worker, nil
}
```

## CloudRunWorker

```go
type CloudRunWorker struct {
    id      string
    outpost string
    issue   string
    status  WorkerStatus
    events  <-chan WorkEvent
    logs    []string
}

func (w *CloudRunWorker) Attach() error {
    return errors.New("Cloud Run workers do not support attach")
}

func (w *CloudRunWorker) Logs() (io.Reader, error) {
    return strings.NewReader(strings.Join(w.logs, "\n")), nil
}
```

## Files

- `internal/outpost/cloudrun.go`

## Dependencies

Depends on: gt-9a2.1 (interfaces), gt-9a2.12 (HTTP client)

 {"id":"gt-9a2.9","title":"Outpost assignment policy: Smart work routing","description":"## Overview

Policy engine for deciding which outpost gets which work.

## Policy Configuration

```yaml
policy:
  # Default order of preference
  default_preference: [local, gce-burst, cloudrun-burst]
  
  # Rules applied in order
  rules:
    # Background work → Cloud Run (cheap)
    - condition: "priority >= P3"
      prefer: cloudrun-burst
      
    # Long tasks → VM (persistent)
    - condition: "estimated_duration > 30m"
      prefer: gce-burst
      
    # Specific epic → specific outpost
    - condition: "epic == gt-abc"
      prefer: local
```

## Implementation

```go
type AssignmentPolicy struct {
    DefaultPreference []string
    Rules             []PolicyRule
}

type PolicyRule struct {
    Condition string   // Simple expression
    Prefer    string   // Outpost name
    Require   string   // Must use this outpost
}

func (p *AssignmentPolicy) SelectOutpost(
    issue Issue, 
    outposts map[string]Outpost,
) Outpost {
    // Check rules in order
    for _, rule := range p.Rules {
        if rule.Matches(issue) {
            if op, ok := outposts[rule.Prefer]; ok {
                if op.ActiveWorkers() < op.MaxWorkers() {
                    return op
                }
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Fall back to default preference
    for _, name := range p.DefaultPreference {
        if op, ok := outposts[name]; ok {
            if op.ActiveWorkers() < op.MaxWorkers() {
                return op
            }
        }
    }
    
    return nil  // All outposts at capacity
}
```

## Condition Language

Simple expressions, not a full DSL:

```
priority >= P3
priority == P0
estimated_duration > 30m
epic == gt-abc
type == bug
label contains "urgent"
```

## Files

- `internal/outpost/policy.go`
- `internal/outpost/condition.go`

 {"id":"gt-9j9","title":"CLI: worker status reporting commands","description":"Worker status reporting CLI for polecats to report progress.

## Commands

### gt worker started
```
gt worker started <issue-id> [-m MESSAGE]
```
Reports work started on issue.

### gt worker progress
```
gt worker progress <issue-id> <0-100> [-m MESSAGE]
```
Reports percentage complete.

### gt worker blocked
```
gt worker blocked <issue-id> <reason> [-m MESSAGE]
```
Reports blocked status with reason.

### gt worker completed
```
gt worker completed <issue-id> [-m MESSAGE]
```
Reports task completion.

### gt worker failed
```
gt worker failed <issue-id> <reason> [-m MESSAGE]
```
Reports task failure.

## Implementation
Each command sends mail to refinery with structured content:
```go
type WorkerStatusReport struct {
    IssueID    string
    Status     string  // started|progress|blocked|completed|failed
    Progress   int     // 0-100 for progress
    Reason     string  // for blocked/failed
    Message    string  // optional detail
    ReportedAt time.Time
}
```

## Message Format
Subject: "[STATUS] <issue-id>: <status>"
Body: JSON-encoded WorkerStatusReport

## Default Recipient
```go
// Determine from context
func getDefaultRecipient() string {
    rig := os.Getenv("GT_RIG")
    if rig != "" {
        return rig + "/refinery"
    }
    return "refinery/"
}
```

## New File
internal/cmd/worker.go

## PGT Reference
gastown-py/src/gastown/cli/worker_cmd.py

- [ ] All 5 commands implemented
- [ ] Status sent as mail to refinery
- [ ] Structured JSON body for parsing
- [ ] Works from polecat session context","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:47:52.795695-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:05:26.715967-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-a95","title":"Refinery background daemon mode","description":"Refinery currently only works in foreground mode. Background daemon is stubbed.

## Current State
manager.go line 128-129:
```go
// Background mode: spawn a new process
// For MVP, we just mark as running - actual daemon implementation in gt-ov2
return nil
```

## Requirements

### 1. Background Process Spawning
```go
func (m *Manager) Start(foreground bool) error {
    if !foreground {
        // Spawn gt refinery start --foreground as subprocess
        cmd := exec.Command(os.Args[0], "refinery", "start", m.rig.Name, "--foreground")
        cmd.Start()  // Don't wait
        // Record PID
    }
}
```

### 2. PID File Management
- Write PID to .gastown/refinery.pid
- Check PID validity on status
- Clean up stale PID files

### 3. Log Output
- Redirect stdout/stderr to .gastown/refinery.log
- Log rotation (optional for MVP)

### 4. Graceful Shutdown
- Handle SIGTERM/SIGINT
- Complete current merge before exit
- Update state to stopped

### 5. Health Check
- Process existence check via kill -0
- Optional: heartbeat file with timestamp

## Files to Modify
- internal/refinery/manager.go: Start(), Status(), process spawning

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] gt refinery start <rig> spawns background process
- [ ] gt refinery status shows running with PID
- [ ] gt refinery stop sends SIGTERM and waits
- [ ] Logs written to .gastown/refinery.log
- [ ] Survives terminal close","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:46:53.366619-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:04:06.010761-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-a9y","title":"File locking for concurrent access","description":"Add file locking for concurrent access safety.

## At-Risk Files
- .gastown/swarms.json (or per-swarm state.json)
- .gastown/refinery.json
- polecats/<name>/state.json
- inbox.jsonl files

## Go File Locking
Use syscall.Flock for advisory locking:
```go
type FileLock struct {
    file *os.File
}

func AcquireLock(path string, timeout time.Duration) (*FileLock, error) {
    f, err := os.OpenFile(path+".lock", os.O_CREATE|os.O_RDWR, 0644)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    // Use syscall.Flock with timeout
}

func (l *FileLock) Release() error
```

## Integration Pattern
```go
func (m *Manager) saveState(ref *Refinery) error {
    lock, err := AcquireLock(m.stateFile(), 5*time.Second)
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("could not acquire lock: %w", err)
    }
    defer lock.Release()
    
    // Read-modify-write cycle
}
```

## New Package
internal/filelock/
├── lock.go     # FileLock, AcquireLock
└── lock_test.go

## Apply To
- internal/refinery/manager.go: loadState/saveState
- internal/cmd/swarm.go: SwarmStore
- internal/mail/mailbox.go: Append, rewrite
- internal/polecat/manager.go: state operations

## Timeout Handling
Default 5 second timeout. Return error if lock not acquired.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Lock files created (.lock extension)
- [ ] Timeout on lock contention
- [ ] All state files protected
- [ ] Locks released on error paths","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:15.641938-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:06:32.441426-08:00"}

No "swarm IDs" - just spawn workers for epic, let merge queue coordinate.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:09.148751-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:26:58.140535-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.196292-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.273483-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.351097-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-kmn.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.431641-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.22","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:36.511124-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-ov2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:51.609649-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-rm3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T00:11:51.69062-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T21:36:32.942855-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"gt-b1g","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T21:36:35.053559-08:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"gt-b3p","title":"Resource Beads: Leases, locks, and quotas","description":"Resource beads represent reserved resources. Types: vm, lock, slot, quota. Fields: holder, expires, renewable. Daemon monitors for expiry and manages contention.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:08:12.745602-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T18:08:12.745602-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-bfd","title":"Keepalive signal from bd/gt commands","description":"Every bd and gt command should touch a keepalive file to signal 'agent is alive/working'.

## Implementation

Touch `<workspace>/.gastown/keepalive.json`:
```json
{"last_command": "bd show gt-99m", "timestamp": "2025-12-18T13:45:00Z"}
```

## Usage by Daemon

- Fresh (< 2 min) → agent is working, skip heartbeat
- Stale (2-5 min) → might be thinking, gentle poke
- Very stale (> 5 min) → likely idle, safe to interrupt

## Benefits

- Zero cost (just file I/O)
- Works during long tool calls
- Doesn't require agent cooperation
- Foundation for smarter backoff strategies","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:26.241957-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:26.241957-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-bfd","depends_on_id":"gt-99m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:46.407664-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-bzd","title":"beads: Stop searching upward when .beads found","description":"## Problem

When running bd commands in a nested directory structure with multiple .beads directories, bd shows a confusing warning:

```
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║ WARNING: 2 beads databases detected in directory hierarchy              ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║ Multiple databases can cause confusion and database pollution.          ║
║                                                                          ║
║   /Users/stevey/gt/gastown/.beads (261 issues)                           ║
║   /Users/stevey/gt/.beads (21 issues)                                    ║
║                                                                          ║
║ WARNING: Not using the closest database! Check your BEADS_DB setting.   ║
║                                                                          ║
║ RECOMMENDED: Consolidate or remove unused databases to avoid confusion. ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

## Why This Is Wrong

In Gas Town, nested .beads directories are **intentional and necessary**:
- Town level: /Users/stevey/gt/.beads (mail, town-level issues)
- Rig level: /Users/stevey/gt/gastown/.beads (gastown project issues)
- Worker level: polecats have their own beads in worktrees

These are **unrelated** beads instances for different scopes. They should never be consolidated.

## Expected Behavior

When bd finds a .beads directory, it should:
1. Use that directory (closest ancestor wins)
2. **Stop searching upward** - do not look for parent .beads directories
3. **No warning** about multiple databases

## Current Behavior

bd searches all the way up to root, finds all .beads directories, and warns about "multiple databases" even though they are separate, intentional instances.

## Fix

In the database discovery code, stop the upward search as soon as a .beads directory is found. The first .beads found is the one to use, and parent directories are out of scope.

## Note

This is a beads issue, filed here for tracking. Should be implemented in the beads codebase.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T19:09:44.295743-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T19:09:44.295743-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-c92","title":"CLI: all command for batch polecat operations","description":"Batch operations across multiple polecats.

## Commands

### gt all start
```
gt all start [--awake-only] [<specs>...]
```
Start sessions for multiple polecats.
- --awake-only: Only start awake polecats
- specs: Polecat names, rig/polecat patterns

### gt all stop
```
gt all stop [<specs>...] [--force]
```
Stop sessions for multiple polecats.

### gt all status
```
gt all status [<specs>...] [--json]
```
Show status of multiple polecats.

### gt all attach
```
gt all attach [<specs>...]
```
Attach to multiple sessions in tmux panes/windows.

### gt all run
```
gt all run <command> [<specs>...]
```
Run command in multiple polecat sessions.

## Spec Patterns
- `Toast`: Specific polecat (in default/current rig)
- `gastown/Toast`: Specific rig/polecat
- `gastown/*`: All polecats in rig
- `*`: All polecats everywhere

## Implementation
```go
func expandSpecs(specs []string, awakeOnly bool) ([]*polecat.Polecat, error) {
    // Expand patterns to list of polecats
}

func runForAll(polecats []*polecat.Polecat, action func(*polecat.Polecat) error) error {
    // Run action for each, collect errors
}
```

## New File
internal/cmd/all.go

## PGT Reference
gastown-py/src/gastown/cli/all_cmd.py

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pattern expansion works
- [ ] Parallel execution where safe
- [ ] Aggregate error reporting
- [ ] --awake-only filter works","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:12.411789-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:05:47.255503-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-caz","title":"Timed Beads: Scheduled recurring work","description":"## Summary

Timed beads wake up periodically and get injected into the ready queue by the daemon.

## Schema Extension

```yaml
id: gt-weekly-sync
type: task  # or sentinel
schedule: "0 9 * * 1"  # cron: Monday 9am
# OR
interval: 24h          # every 24 hours
tier: haiku            # cheap model for routine checks
next_run: 2025-12-20T09:00:00Z
```

## Daemon Integration

Daemon heartbeat loop:
1. Check timed beads where `next_run <= now`
2. For each due bead:
   - Inject into ready queue (set status to open if needed)
   - Update `next_run` based on schedule/interval
3. Witnesses pick up work via `bd ready`

## Use Cases

- Weekly team sync reminders
- Daily health checks
- Periodic cleanup tasks
- Scheduled reports

## Interaction with Pinned Beads

A pinned bead can be timed - it wakes up periodically but never closes.
This is how you model "background services" in Gas Town.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:39.665294-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:39.665294-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-cik","title":"Overseer Crew: User-managed persistent workspaces","description":"## Overview

Crew workers are the overseer's (human's) personal workspaces within a rig. Unlike polecats which are witness-managed and ephemeral, crew workers are:

- **Persistent**: Not auto-garbage-collected
- **User-managed**: Overseer controls lifecycle
- **Long-lived identities**: dave, emma, fred - recognizable names
- **Gas Town integrated**: Mail, handoff mechanics work
- **Tmux optional**: Can work in terminal directly

## Directory Structure

```
<rig>/
  polecats/       # Managed workers (witness controls)
  refinery/       # Merge queue processor
  witness/        # Pit boss
  crew/           # Overseer's personal workspaces
    dave/         # Full clone, persistent
    emma/         # Full clone, persistent
    fred/         # Full clone, persistent
```

## Key Differences from Polecats

- Location: crew/ instead of polecats/
- Lifecycle: User-managed, not witness-managed
- Auto-cleanup: Never (polecats auto-cleanup on swarm land)
- Issue assignment: Optional (polecats require it)
- Tmux: Optional (polecats require it)
- Mail & Handoff: Yes for both
- Identity: Persistent (polecats are ephemeral)

## CLI Commands

- gt crew add <name> [--rig <rig>] - Create crew workspace
- gt crew list [--rig <rig>] - List crew workspaces
- gt crew at <rig>/<name> - Attach to workspace (start session)
- gt crew attach <name> - Attach (infer rig from cwd)
- gt crew refresh <name> - Handoff + restart (context cycling)
- gt crew remove <name> [--force] - Remove workspace
- gt crew status [<name>] - Show workspace status

## Design Notes

- Crew workers use full git clones (not worktrees)
- Optional beads integration via BEADS_DIR
- Mail-to-self handoff works for context cycling
- No witness monitoring or nudging
- No automatic issue assignment required

## Background

Users often maintain separate repo clones for serial agent work. This is tedious to set up manually.
